channel.title: Oil-Immersed Transformers on Zan
channel.description: Recent content in Oil-Immersed Transformers on Zan

item.title: 110 kV Oil-Immersed Transformer
item.description:
This 110 kV oil-immersed transformer is designed for high-availability grid duty rather than voltage class alone. Its liquid-immersed core-and-coil system uses insulating oil for both dielectric strength and heat dissipation, helping maintain stable electrical and thermal behavior under continuous service. The platform is suitable for public utilities, urban substations, and industrial load centers where interruption costs are high and reliability targets are strict.

Project configurations can be tailored around cooling arrangements, tank design, and de-energized or on-load tap-changing strategies according to network regulation requirements. With a focus on moisture control, winding mechanical strength, and validated factory testing such as impulse and temperature-rise tests, this series supports dependable long-term operation, reduced outage risk, and stronger lifecycle value for international EPCs and power asset owners.

item.title: 220 kV Oil-Immersed Transformer
item.description:
The 220 kV oil-immersed transformer is positioned for backbone transmission service where large power blocks must be transferred efficiently and securely. Compared with lower voltage classes, this category typically requires stricter design margins in insulation coordination, thermal management, short-circuit mechanical integrity, and condition monitoring readiness, because it operates deeper in the transmission backbone.

Depending on system architecture, the platform can be delivered in more complex arrangements such as three-winding or autotransformer configurations with on-load voltage regulation, supporting system-level grid control instead of simple end-point distribution. Through disciplined material selection, manufacturing quality control, and comprehensive test verification, this series helps utilities improve network resilience, reduce forced-outage exposure, and protect long-term infrastructure returns.

item.title: 3D Wound-Core Transformer
item.description:
The defining feature of this model is its 3D wound-core geometry, which is intended to create a more symmetrical three-phase magnetic circuit than conventional planar stacked cores. In practical engineering terms, that structure is commonly associated with lower exciting current, improved flux distribution, reduced no-load loss, and lower vibration/noise behavior, especially in distribution networks that prioritize power quality and acoustic control.

Actual performance benefits depend on rating, core material grade, winding design, and manufacturing consistency, so the most accurate technical positioning is a structure-led efficiency and low-noise optimization solution rather than a universal fixed-loss claim. For urban distribution systems, commercial facilities, and noise-sensitive installations, this transformer provides a strong balance of operational reliability, energy performance, and installation friendliness.

item.title: Amorphous 3D Wound-Core Transformer
item.description:
This transformer family integrates two efficiency-oriented design paths: amorphous alloy core technology for no-load-loss reduction and 3D wound-core geometry for improved three-phase magnetic balance. The combination is particularly valuable in networks with long energized periods and frequent light-load operation, where baseline losses have a large impact on annual energy cost and total ownership economics.

As with any engineered product, realized savings depend on the exact rating, design execution, and manufacturing quality, so procurement decisions should rely on certified loss sheets and test documentation for the offered model. Positioned correctly, this series delivers a credible value proposition of stronger energy-saving performance, stable oil-immersed reliability, and improved long-term operating efficiency for utilities, industrial users, and international EPC projects.

item.title: S13 Oil-Immersed Transformer
item.description:
S13 is widely recognized as a practical and proven low-loss oil-immersed distribution platform. Typical designs include high-permeability grain-oriented silicon steel cores, optimized winding structures, and fully sealed tanks, with a focus on reducing no-load loss and no-load current while maintaining reliable day-to-day operation in common distribution duty.

For retrofit programs replacing older-generation units, S13 provides a low-risk path to better efficiency without unnecessary system complexity. It is suitable for industrial parks, public infrastructure, and mixed commercial distribution systems that require stable supply quality and predictable operating cost. During quotation and technical clarification, guaranteed loss values and key electrical parameters should always be confirmed against the specific nameplate and certified test report.

item.title: S20 Oil-Immersed Transformer
item.description:
S20 can be understood as an efficiency-upgraded sealed oil-immersed distribution series for buyers who need better loss performance than mainstream low-loss options while keeping a balanced investment profile. Common product architecture emphasizes quality silicon steel core materials, compact winding design, full sealing, and low-noise operation to support reliable voltage service with lower annual energy waste.

This positioning makes S20 well suited to manufacturing facilities, utility distribution upgrades, and commercial infrastructure with long energized hours and structured energy-management targets. The commercial value is strongest when evaluated on total lifecycle cost rather than purchase price alone. Final technical commitments should be validated through model-specific certified loss sheets, temperature-rise data, and agreed testing standards.

item.title: S22 Oil-Immersed Transformer
item.description:
S22 is typically treated as the high-efficiency tier within the S13/S20/S22 sealed oil-immersed distribution family. Its value proposition centers on stronger loss control under real operating conditions, which becomes increasingly important for projects with long annual service hours and strict operating-expenditure targets. For such applications, improved efficiency directly contributes to lower energy cost and stronger long-term asset performance.

The series is an appropriate fit for modern industrial parks, smart distribution networks, and ESG-driven infrastructure programs that prioritize lifecycle energy outcomes. As with all distribution transformers, model name alone does not define guaranteed loss, impedance, or vector group values, so procurement decisions should be based on the exact technical offer and certified test documentation. In practical sales terms, S22 is best positioned as a premium energy-efficient sealed oil-immersed distribution solution.
